title: The Columbian Exchange, Spanish exploration, and conquest
description: Practice your historical skills in this quiz about the Columbian Exchange.
topic_path: The Columbian Exchange, Spanish exploration, and conquest

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Colón [Columbus] and his crew did not voyage alone. They were accompanied by a menagerie of insects, plants, mammals, and microorganisms . . . European expeditions brought cattle, sheep, and horses, along with crops like sugarcane (originally from New Guinea), wheat (from the Middle East), bananas (from Africa), and coffee (also from Africa) . . . "

-From Charles C. Mann, historian, *1493: Uncovering the New World Columbus Created*, 2011. 

**Which of the following processes is Mann describing?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Columbian Exchange
The Protestant Reformation
The transatlantic slave trade
The Little Ice Age

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Colón [Columbus] and his crew did not voyage alone. They were accompanied by a menagerie of insects, plants, mammals, and microorganisms . . . European expeditions brought cattle, sheep, and horses, along with crops like sugarcane (originally from New Guinea), wheat (from the Middle East), bananas (from Africa), and coffee (also from Africa) . . . "

-From Charles C. Mann, historian, *1493: Uncovering the New World Columbus Created*, 2011. 

**How was the population of the Old World affected by the process Mann describes?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Population decreased as new diseases like smallpox were introduced
Population decreased as new crops were introduced, causing people to die from new allergies
Population increased as new crops were introduced, improving diets and increasing life expectancy
Population increased as herbal cures found in the New World helped to address major diseases in the Old World

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Colón [Columbus] and his crew did not voyage alone. They were accompanied by a menagerie of insects, plants, mammals, and microorganisms . . . European expeditions brought cattle, sheep, and horses, along with crops like sugarcane (originally from New Guinea), wheat (from the Middle East), bananas (from Africa), and coffee (also from Africa) . . . "

-From Charles C. Mann, historian, *1493: Uncovering the New World Columbus Created*, 2011. 

**Mann describes several commodities that were taken from Europe and Africa to the Americas. Which of the following were taken from the Americas to Europe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Gold and silver
Sheep and cattle
Apples and peaches
Dogs and cats

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

>"Colón [Columbus] and his crew did not voyage alone. They were accompanied by a menagerie of insects, plants, mammals, and microorganisms . . . European expeditions brought cattle, sheep, and horses, along with crops like sugarcane (originally from New Guinea), wheat (from the Middle East), bananas (from Africa), and coffee (also from Africa) . . . "

-From Charles C. Mann, historian, *1493: Uncovering the New World Columbus Created*, 2011. 

**Which of the following economic shifts resulted most directly from the trends described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The emergence of the Silk Road, a trade route between Europe and Asia
European economies shifted from a feudal economy to a capitalist economy
Native American economies shifted from capitalist economies to feudal economies
A religious caste system in the New World

assessment_items.question_content:
![Print depicting a restored caravel](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a45ce442daa4f1aa38625c7e1fc699c.png)
Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bras_de_Oliveira_Caravel_with_oars.png)

**What was important about the development of vessels such as the one depicted above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They allowed Portuguese and Spanish navigators to sail across oceans rather than only along the coast
They were capable of carrying more than 300 people, making it easy to start colonial settlements
They were the first ships to have cannons on board, leading Portugal to excel in naval warfare
They were specially designed to carry enslaved laborers on their lower decks

assessment_items.question_content:
![Print depicting a restored caravel](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a45ce442daa4f1aa38625c7e1fc699c.png)
Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bras_de_Oliveira_Caravel_with_oars.png)

**In addition to new maritime technologies, what other innovations helped to spur European exploration in the Americas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: New methods for organizing international trade, like the joint-stock company
New forms of communication, like the telegraph
New democratic governments, like the one instituted after the French Revolution
New methods of preserving food for long journeys, like pickling

assessment_items.question_content:
![Print depicting a restored caravel](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6a45ce442daa4f1aa38625c7e1fc699c.png)
Image credit: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bras_de_Oliveira_Caravel_with_oars.png)

**How did New World colonization, enabled by the vessel above, affect the economy of Europe?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It brought a great deal of gold and silver into Europe, stimulating the banking industry and promoting Europe’s transition to capitalism
It caused rampant deflation, leading to many bankruptcies among borrowers
It devastated the business of European farmers who grew tomatoes and potatoes, as New World imports of these crops undercut their sales
It allowed banking firms to have more power than European monarchies, which were deeply in debt

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the excerpt and answer the question below.

>“Where colonies are founded for a public-weal, they may continue in better obedience and become more industrious than where private men are absolute backers of a voyage. Men of better behavior and quality will engage themselves in a public service, which carries more reputation with it, than a private, which is for the most part ignominious in the end, because it is presumed to aim at a profit and is subject to rivalry, fraud, and envy, and when it is at the greatest height of fortune can hardly be tolerated because of the jealousy of the state. . . .”

>-Richard Hakluyt, *Reasons for Raising a Fund to Settle America On the Value of Colonies to England,* 1605

**Which of the following groups of people would have been most likely to oppose Hakluyt’s views in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: owners of joint-stock companies
leaders of the Church of England
privateers preying on Spanish ships
navigators looking for the northwest passage

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Image of women covered in spots](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f48f4d59043a357b57d58337bc144b3.jpg)

> -Source: Bernardino de Sahagún, [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FlorentineCodex_BK12_F54_smallpox.jpg) 1576

**Which of the following does this image most likely depict?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Native Americans suffering from microbial diseases introduced by Spanish conquistadores
Spanish conquistadores’ wives, who were  infected with smallpox in the New World
Spanish women who were dying at unprecedented rates from the Bubonic Plague
Native Americans who gave smallpox to Spanish conquistadores upon their arrival in the New World

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Image of women covered in spots](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f48f4d59043a357b57d58337bc144b3.jpg)

> -Source: Bernardino de Sahagún, [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FlorentineCodex_BK12_F54_smallpox.jpg) 1576

**The image most closely reflects which of the following demographic changes in the Spanish colonies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a decrease in Native Americans as smallpox spread, but an increase in Spaniards as they continued to colonize
a decrease in Native Americans and Spaniards as smallpox infected both Native Americans and Spaniards
a decrease in enslaved people of African descent as smallpox spread, but an increase in the number of Spaniards as they were immune
an increase in interracial marriages between Spaniards, African people, and Native Americans to pass on immunity

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Image of women covered in spots](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f48f4d59043a357b57d58337bc144b3.jpg)

> -Source: Bernardino de Sahagún, [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FlorentineCodex_BK12_F54_smallpox.jpg) 1576

**In addition to the effect depicted in the image, which of the following was another negative environmental impact of Spanish colonization in the Americas?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The introduction of new animals negatively affected Native American crops
The introduction of new plants gave Native Americans new allergies
The introduction of potatoes led to widespread famine
The introduction of sheep and cattle led to a dramatic increase in the wolf population

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Image of women covered in spots](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f48f4d59043a357b57d58337bc144b3.jpg)

> -Source: Bernardino de Sahagún, [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FlorentineCodex_BK12_F54_smallpox.jpg) 1576

 **Which of the following most directly led to the circumstances illustrated by the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Spanish exploration of the Americas
Native American immunity to disease
conflicts between European explorers
contaminated food brought from Europe

assessment_items.question_content:
Examine the image and answer the question below. 

![Image of women covered in spots](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6f48f4d59043a357b57d58337bc144b3.jpg)

> -Source: Bernardino de Sahagún, [Wikimedia Commons,](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:FlorentineCodex_BK12_F54_smallpox.jpg) 1576

**Which of the following developments is the most direct effect of the situation portrayed in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Spanish increased their use of enslaved Africans as a labor force
increased opposition from the Spanish clergy to using Native Americans as labor
increased opposition from the Spanish crown to Spanish colonization
the enslaved African population decreased as the Spanish moved to end colonization
